info.title: search-a-licious API
info.description:
The Search-a-licious API helps you quickly build applications with search capabilities.

You can find the API documentation here.

See also the available [web-components](../static/web-components.html) to build your search interface.

See the [project](https://github.com/openfoodfacts/search-a-licious/) for more information.

get.description: Fetch a document from Elasticsearch with specific ID.

parameters.name: index_id

schema.description:
Index ID to use for the search, if not provided, the default index is used.
        If there is only one index, this parameter is not needed.
schema.title: Index Id

parameters.description:
Index ID to use for the search, if not provided, the default index is used.
        If there is only one index, this parameter is not needed.

parameters.name: q

schema.description:
The search query, it supports Lucene search query
syntax (https://lucene.apache.org/core/3_6_0/queryparsersyntax.html). Words
that are not recognized by the lucene query parser are searched as full text
search.

Example: `categories_tags:"en:beverages" strawberry brands:"casino"` query use a
filter clause for categories and brands and look for "strawberry" in multiple
fields.

The query is optional, but `sort_by` value must then be provided.
schema.title: Q

parameters.description:
The search query, it supports Lucene search query
syntax (https://lucene.apache.org/core/3_6_0/queryparsersyntax.html). Words
that are not recognized by the lucene query parser are searched as full text
search.

Example: `categories_tags:"en:beverages" strawberry brands:"casino"` query use a
filter clause for categories and brands and look for "strawberry" in multiple
fields.

The query is optional, but `sort_by` value must then be provided.

parameters.name: langs

schema.description:
List of languages we want to support during search.
This list should include the user expected language, and additional languages (such
as english for example).

This is currently used for language-specific subfields to choose in which
subfields we're searching in.

If not provided, `['en']` is used.
schema.title: Langs

parameters.description:
List of languages we want to support during search.
This list should include the user expected language, and additional languages (such
as english for example).

This is currently used for language-specific subfields to choose in which
subfields we're searching in.

If not provided, `['en']` is used.

parameters.name: page_size

schema.description: Number of results to return per page.
schema.title: Page Size

parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.

parameters.name: fields

schema.description: List of fields to include in the response. All other fields will be ignored.
schema.title: Fields

parameters.description: List of fields to include in the response. All other fields will be ignored.

parameters.name: sort_by

schema.description:
Field name to use to sort results, the field should exist
and be sortable. If it is not provided, results are sorted by descending relevance score.

If you put a minus before the name, the results will be sorted by descending order.

If the field name match a known script (defined in your configuration),
it will be use for sorting.

In this case you also need to provide additional parameters corresponding to your script parameters.
If a script needs parameters, you can only use the POST method.

Beware that this may have a big [impact on performance][perf_link]

Also bare in mind [privacy considerations][privacy_link] if your script parameters contains sensible data.

[perf_link]: https://openfoodfacts.github.io/search-a-licious/users/how-to-use-scripts/#performance-considerations
[privacy_link]: https://openfoodfacts.github.io/search-a-licious/users/how-to-use-scripts/#performance-considerations
schema.title: Sort By

parameters.description:
Field name to use to sort results, the field should exist
and be sortable. If it is not provided, results are sorted by descending relevance score.

If you put a minus before the name, the results will be sorted by descending order.

If the field name match a known script (defined in your configuration),
it will be use for sorting.

In this case you also need to provide additional parameters corresponding to your script parameters.
If a script needs parameters, you can only use the POST method.

Beware that this may have a big [impact on performance][perf_link]

Also bare in mind [privacy considerations][privacy_link] if your script parameters contains sensible data.

[perf_link]: https://openfoodfacts.github.io/search-a-licious/users/how-to-use-scripts/#performance-considerations
[privacy_link]: https://openfoodfacts.github.io/search-a-licious/users/how-to-use-scripts/#performance-considerations

parameters.name: facets

schema.description:
Name of facets to return in the response as a comma-separated value.
            If None (default) no facets are returned.
schema.title: Facets

parameters.description:
Name of facets to return in the response as a comma-separated value.
            If None (default) no facets are returned.

parameters.name: charts

schema.description:
Name of vega representations to return in the response.
            Can be distribution chart or scatter plot
schema.title: Charts

parameters.description:
Name of vega representations to return in the response.
            Can be distribution chart or scatter plot

parameters.name: index_id

schema.description:
Index ID to use for the search, if not provided, the default index is used.
        If there is only one index, this parameter is not needed.
schema.title: Index Id

parameters.description:
Index ID to use for the search, if not provided, the default index is used.
        If there is only one index, this parameter is not needed.

parameters.name: taxonomy_names

schema.description: Name(s) of the taxonomy to search in, as a comma-separated value.
schema.title: Taxonomy Names

parameters.description: Name(s) of the taxonomy to search in, as a comma-separated value.

parameters.name: lang

schema.description: Language to search in, defaults to 'en'.
schema.title: Lang

parameters.description: Language to search in, defaults to 'en'.

parameters.name: fuzziness

schema.description: Fuzziness level to use, default to no fuzziness.
schema.title: Fuzziness

parameters.description: Fuzziness level to use, default to no fuzziness.

        If there is only one index, this parameter is not needed.

sort_params.title: Sort Params
sort_params.description:
Additional parameters when using  a sort script in sort_by.
            If the sort script needs parameters, you can only be used the POST method.

index_id.title: Index Id
index_id.description:
Index ID to use for the search, if not provided, the default index is used.
        If there is only one index, this parameter is not needed.
